muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server --use-datastar
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /events Stream(ctx, elements, signal)" -}}{{- .Result -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.25
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

type Count struct {
	Count int `json:"count"`
}

// Stream patches elements and signals over one event stream: the elements
// callback renders the template as a datastar-patch-elements frame; the signal
// callback marshals the result as an inline datastar-patch-signals frame, with
// onlyIfMissing emitted because the second argument is true.
func (Server) Stream(ctx context.Context, elements func(data string) error, signal func(data Count, onlyIfMissing bool) error) {
	_ = elements("hi")
	_ = signal(Count{Count: 5}, true)
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestStream(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/events", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Code, http.StatusOK)
	}
	if ct := rec.Header().Get("Content-Type"); ct != "text/event-stream" {
		t.Fatalf("content-type = %q, want text/event-stream", ct)
	}
	got := rec.Body.String()
	for _, want := range []string{
		"event: datastar-patch-elements\n",
		"data: elements hi\n",
		"event: datastar-patch-signals\n",
		"data: signals {\"count\":5}\n",
		"data: onlyIfMissing true\n",
	} {
		if !strings.Contains(got, want) {
			t.Fatalf("body = %q, want it to contain %q", got, want)
		}
	}
}
